title: referrals — kill switches
description: Operational kill switches distinct from the tier-based `features.json` gates. These are for emergency-off / staged rollout / per-tenant ops interventions. Every flag is ON in normal production; off flips are ops decisions.

flags.description: Master toggle for the claim endpoint. Off = GET /api/v1/referrals/claim/{code} returns 503 with a maintenance banner. Emergency-only (used during DB migrations of the referrals table).

FraudDetector runs synchronously at claim time. Off = async fallback (DetectReferralFraudJob dispatched instead). Sync is safer (blocks bad claims before persistence) but slower — 250ms typical. Test envs flip off for latency benchmarks.
Off = MaterializeVestedRewardsJob no-ops. Rewards stay in status='vested' until re-enabled. Used when Finance module is unstable to avoid backing up its queue.
Off = finance::RefundIssued / ChargebackFiled do NOT cascade to clawback. Admins must clawback manually. Emergency-only if the cascade is misbehaving.
Off = ReferralSignedUp / ReferralVested do NOT fire marketing events. Ad-network optimisation pauses. Used when marketing module is unstable.
Off = NotifyReferralInvitationJob no-ops. POST /me/invitations still succeeds (records the event) but nothing is dispatched. Used when notifications module is unstable.
Off = ReconcileProgramBudgetJob + CheckReferralVestingJob + ExpireStaleReferralsJob skip. Only flip off for maintenance windows.
Off = ExpireStaleReferralsJob does NOT auto-transition claimed -> expired. Admins expire manually via the ledger.
Off = referrals do NOT capture attribution_snapshot at claim time. Downstream marketing events lose attribution data. Emergency-only if attribution module is corrupt.
